content.claim: Perception of school value diverges from revealed preference: survey research found that in villages with schools, 81%+ of residents consider the school difficult to do without, but only 17% of residents in municipalities without elementary schools consider the absence a major shortcoming, suggesting school value may be partly anticipatory/symbolic rather than reflecting actual dissatisfaction once closure occurs.
content.context: Fresh live research through the Education finance lens
limitations: Survey measures stated preference, not behavioral response
Cannot determine whether low post-closure dissatisfaction reflects adaptation or selection bias (dissatisfied families may have already left)
Limited sample size and geographic scope not specified
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
